subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Region R Bounded by ln x and 5-x — Area, Square Cross Section Volume & Horizontal Dividing Line
stimulus: Let $R$ be the region in the first quadrant bounded by the $x$-axis and the graphs of $y = \ln x$ and $y = 5 - x$, as shown in the figure.

parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Intersection at $(A, B) \approx (3.693, 1.307)$. Area $= \int_0^B [(5-y) - e^y]\, dy \approx 2.986$ (or equivalently $\int_1^A \ln x\, dx + \int_A^5 (5-x)\, dx \approx 2.986$).

scoring_points.description: Correct integrand: uses horizontal slices $\int_0^B [(5-y) - e^y]\, dy$ (or vertical slices with two integrals); intersection at $(A, B) \approx (3.693, 1.307)$

required_elements: Correct integrand: uses horizontal slices $\int_0^B [(5-y) - e^y]\, dy$ (or vertical slices with two integrals); intersection at $(A, B) \approx (3.693, 1.307)$

parts.prompt: Region $R$ is the base of a solid. For the solid, each cross section perpendicular to the $x$-axis is a square. Write, but do not evaluate, an expression involving one or more integrals that gives the volume of the solid.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Volume $= \int_1^A (\ln x)^2\, dx + \int_A^5 (5-x)^2\, dx$ where $A \approx 3.693$ (do not evaluate).

scoring_points.description: Identifies that cross-section area = (side)² where side depends on region boundary

required_elements: Identifies that cross-section area = (side)² where side depends on region boundary

parts.prompt: The horizontal line $y = k$ divides $R$ into two regions of equal area. Write, but do not solve, an equation involving one or more integrals whose solution gives the value of $k$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\int_0^k [(5-y) - e^y]\, dy = \frac{1}{2}(2.986)$ (do not solve for $k$).
